tree.setup: You arrive at a hotel to check in.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 您好，欢迎。请问您预订了吗？
turns.text_pinyin: nín hǎo, huānyíng. qǐngwèn nín yùdìng le ma?
turns.text_en: Hello, welcome. Do you have a reservation?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Say yes, you have a reservation under your name.

options.text_zh: 当然可以！祝你生日快乐！我带什么来好呢？
options.text_pinyin: dāngrán kěyǐ! zhù nǐ shēngrì kuàilè! wǒ dài shénme lái hǎo ne?
options.text_en: Of course! Happy birthday! What should I bring?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to say yes, you have a reservation under your name, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 你好，我上午在这里喝咖啡，好像把背包忘在这儿了。
options.text_pinyin: nǐ hǎo, wǒ shàngwǔ zài zhèlǐ hē kāfēi, hǎoxiàng bǎ bēibāo wàng zài zhèr le.
options.text_en: Hi, I was having coffee here this morning and I think I left my backpack here.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to say yes, you have a reservation under your name, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_pinyin: shì de, wǒ yùdìng le. wǒ xìng lǐ.
options.text_en: Yes, I have a reservation. My surname is Li.
options.feedback: Clear and professional. 我姓... is the standard way to give your surname.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好的，找到了。两个晚上，对吗？
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo de, zhǎodào le. liǎng gè wǎnshang, duì ma?
turns.text_en: OK, found it. Two nights, correct?

options.text_zh: 十二点吧。你想吃什么都行。
options.text_pinyin: shí'èr diǎn ba. nǐ xiǎng chī shénme dōu xíng.
options.text_en: How about noon. Whatever you want is fine.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to confirm and ask about breakfast, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 当然可以！祝你生日快乐！我带什么来好呢？
options.text_pinyin: dāngrán kěyǐ! zhù nǐ shēngrì kuàilè! wǒ dài shénme lái hǎo ne?
options.text_en: Of course! Happy birthday! What should I bring?
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to confirm and ask about breakfast, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_pinyin: duì de. qǐngwèn yǒu zǎocān ma?
options.feedback: Confirms and asks a practical follow-up. Well done.

tree.cultural_note: At Chinese hotels, 我姓... (wǒ xìng, 'my surname is...') is preferred over giving your full name. Surnames come first in Chinese — 李明 is surname 李, given name 明.
